answer:
In the Smṛti corpus (e.g., *Manusmṛti*, *Aṅgirasa Smṛti*, *Kātyāyana Smṛti*), the *guru* (literally "heavy one," denoting weighty authority) is primarily the ritual preceptor and teacher of sacred knowledge (*śruti* and smṛti) for a *brahmachārin* (student celibate). He performs the sacraments (*saṃskāras*) from conception onward, nourishes the pupil with food, and imparts Vedic learning (even a little qualifies one as *guru*); he is thus more venerable than the householder's fire (*garhapatya*) or even the mother.[^1][^2][^3] The *guru* also chastises the self-controlled ethically, akin to the king for the wicked or Yama for hidden sins, purifying both parties when done per *dharma*.[^4] Students must observe strict etiquette: lower seat/bed, inferior food/dress, daily salutations (clasp feet of *guru* and wife), service to extract knowledge "like water from digging," no imitation of gait/speech, and reverence extended to *guru*'s kin.[^5] Defiling the *guru*'s bed is among the "five fallen" sins.[^6]

Across Old Javanese (Kawi) ethical texts in the corpus (e.g., *Gaṇapatitattva*, *Vṛhaspatitattva*), *guru*-devotion (*gurubhakti*) marks the ideal disciple for esoteric knowledge (*tattvajñāna*): one of conquered senses, faith, vows, righteousness, and self-control in speech, worthy of *Gaṇapati*'s distinguishing wisdom.[^7] No comparable systematic treatment of *guru*-etiquette or definition appears in other Kawi works like *Kunjarakarna Dharmakathana*, *Kalpabuddha*, or *San Hyan* series (searches yielded no matches for "guru," "teacher," or "preceptor").

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, [Chapter 2, p-36a740](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-36a740).
[^2]: *Manusmṛti*, [Chapter 2, p-3faca8](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-3faca8).
[^3]: *Manusmṛti*, [Chapter 2, p-3e3f4a](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-3e3f4a).
[^4]: *Aṅgirasa Smṛti*, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-1de5ee).
[^5]: *Manusmṛti*, [Chapter 2](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/02/translation/#p-fe7be5) (representative etiquette passage).
[^6]: *Aṅgirasa Smṛti*, [Chapter 1, p-cafcb4](/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-cafcb4).
[^7]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-33578f).
